member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Tools.AssemblyHandler
member.summary: Handles assembly-related operations such as loading, scanning for extensions, and managing driver configurations. Core partial class containing fields, properties, and constructor.
AssemblyHandler partial class - Driver Package Tracking Tracks which NuGet packages provide which drivers.
AssemblyHandler partial class - Helper Methods and Utilities
AssemblyHandler partial class - Assembly Loading Methods
AssemblyHandler partial class - NuGet Search, Download, and Package Operations Implements the new IAssemblyHandler NuGet members.
AssemblyHandler partial class - NuGet Source Management Manages custom NuGet package sources with persistence.
AssemblyHandler partial class - Assembly Scanning Methods
AssemblyHandler partial class - Load Statistics Tracks assembly loading performance and outcomes.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Tools.ClassCreator
member.summary: Partial class for documentation, utilities, and advanced features
Enhanced ClassCreator with modular architecture using partial classes and helper pattern. Provides comprehensive class generation capabilities with separation of concerns.
Partial class for database-related class generation functionality
Partial class for DLL creation and compilation functionality
Entity class generation using EntitiesExtensions for type conversion
Partial class extending ClassCreator with POCO to Entity generation capabilities. Supports namespace scanning, runtime type loading, and datasource integration.
Partial class for testing and validation functionality
Partial class for Web API generation functionality

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheManager
member.summary: Advanced cache manager with pluggable providers, stale-while-revalidate support, scoped provider access, and SLO-ready statistics. Supports multiple cache backends including In-Memory, MemoryCache, Redis, and custom providers.
Extension methods for cache operations to provide additional functionality.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheManager.Initialize(TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheConfiguration,TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheProviderType,System.Nullable{TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheProviderType})
member.summary: Initializes the cache manager with the specified configuration.

member.summary: Sets a custom cache provider as the primary provider.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheManager.GetOrCreateAsync``1(System.String,System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets a cached value or creates it using the provided factory function. Uses an explicit hit/miss contract — null, 0, false, and empty collections are valid cached values and are NOT treated as cache misses.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheManager.GetOrCreateWithStaleAsync``1(System.String,System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets a cached value or creates it using stale-while-revalidate semantics. Returns a (possibly stale) cached value immediately; if the entry is older than , a background refresh is triggered so the next caller will receive fresh data. The hard TTL () is when the item is fully evicted and the next call will block on the factory.
member.param: Async factory invoked on misses and background refreshes.
How long a value is considered fresh (serve without refresh).
How long the value stays in cache after softTtl passes.
Cancellation token (only used for blocking path).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheManager.GetOrCreate``1(System.String,System.Func{``0},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Gets a cached value or creates it using the provided factory function (synchronous version).
member.typeparam: The type of the cached value.
member.param: Factory function to create the value if not cached.
member.returns: The cached or newly created value.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheScope
member.summary:
An isolated, instance-based cache backed by a dedicated .
Use to obtain a scope. Each scope has its own provider (and therefore its own statistics, key namespace, and eviction policy), so components like ProxyDataSource can have per-datasource caches without sharing the global instance.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.CacheScope.GetOrCreateAsync``1(System.String,System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Fetch-or-create with stale-while-revalidate semantics.
If the key exists and is within , return it.
If the key is older than but the hard TTL () has not expired, return the stale value and trigger a background refresh.
On a hard miss, invoke synchronously.

member.summary: Simple get-or-create without stale semantics.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheConnectionHelperExample
member.summary:
Comprehensive example demonstrating the usage of Cache Connection Helpers in the Beep Data Management Engine.
This class provides complete examples of: - Registering cache connection configurations - Creating cache data source connections - Working with different cache types (in-memory, distributed, hybrid) - Configuring connection properties for various cache systems - Best practices for cache configuration and usage
The Cache Connection Helpers support various caching technologies including: - Built-in Beep InMemoryCache - Microsoft Extensions Memory Cache - Redis distributed cache - Hazelcast in-memory data grid - Apache Ignite cache - Hybrid and multi-level caching systems

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample
member.summary:
Comprehensive example demonstrating the InMemoryCacheDataSource functionality.
This class provides complete examples of: - Initializing and configuring InMemoryCacheDataSource for in-memory data operations - Creating and managing entities with automatic schema discovery - Performing full CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) on cached data - Advanced filtering and querying with multiple operators (equals, contains, greater than, etc.) - Paging support for large datasets with configurable page sizes - Entity structure management including custom entity creation - Integration with CacheManager for advanced caching scenarios - Performance testing and benchmarking of cache operations - Statistics collection and monitoring of cache performance - Proper resource cleanup and disposal patterns
The InMemoryCacheDataSource serves as a high-performance, thread-safe in-memory data store that implements the full IDataSource interface, making it compatible with the entire Beep data management ecosystem while providing microsecond-level response times.
Key Features Demonstrated: - Thread-safe concurrent operations using ConcurrentDictionary - Automatic entity structure inference from data objects - Support for complex filtering with multiple conditions - Integration with external cache providers for persistence - Memory-efficient data storage and retrieval - Comprehensive error handling and logging

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample._cacheDataSource
member.summary: The main InMemoryCacheDataSource instance used for all demonstration operations. Provides in-memory data storage with full IDataSource interface compatibility.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample._dmeEditor
member.summary: Data Management Editor interface providing access to configuration, logging, and other core Beep framework functionality.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample._logger
member.summary: Logger interface for recording operations, errors, and performance metrics throughout the example execution.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.#ctor(TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor,TheTechIdea.Beep.Logger.IDMLogger)
member.summary:
Initializes a new instance of the CacheMemoryDataSourceExample class.
Sets up the example environment with the provided editor and logger instances, preparing for demonstration of InMemoryCacheDataSource capabilities.
member.param: The data management editor for framework integration
The logger for recording operations and events

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.RunBasicExample
member.summary:
Runs a comprehensive basic example demonstrating core InMemoryCacheDataSource functionality.
This method orchestrates a complete demonstration including: 1. Data source initialization and connection establishment 2. Sample data creation with multiple entity types (Users, Products) 3. Full CRUD operations with error handling and validation 4. Advanced filtering and querying capabilities 5. Entity management and structure discovery 6. Proper resource cleanup and disposal
The example uses realistic business data (users, products, orders) to show practical usage patterns and best practices for the InMemoryCacheDataSource.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.InitializeCacheDataSource
member.summary:
Initializes and configures the InMemoryCacheDataSource for demonstration.
This method: - Creates a new InMemoryCacheDataSource instance with proper configuration - Sets up error handling and logging integration - Establishes the connection to the in-memory cache - Validates the connection status and reports results
The data source is configured for in-memory cache operations with automatic entity discovery and thread-safe concurrent access.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.CreateSampleData
member.summary:
Creates comprehensive sample data to demonstrate various data operations.
This method populates the cache with: - User entities with diverse attributes (name, email, age, department) - Product entities with pricing and inventory information - Realistic business data for testing filtering and querying
Each insert operation is validated and logged, demonstrating proper error handling and success verification patterns.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateCrudOperations
member.summary:
Demonstrates comprehensive CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations.
This method showcases: - Reading all entities from a collection - Updating existing entities with new information - Deleting entities by primary key - Verifying changes with subsequent read operations - Proper error handling and status validation
Each operation is logged and verified to ensure data integrity and demonstrate proper usage patterns for the InMemoryCacheDataSource.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateQuerying
member.summary:
Demonstrates advanced filtering and querying capabilities.
This method showcases: - Single-condition filtering (department equals "Engineering") - Multi-condition filtering (category and price range) - Various filter operators (equals, less than, contains, etc.) - Paged result retrieval with configurable page sizes - Complex query patterns for business scenarios
The filtering system supports all standard comparison operators and can handle multiple conditions with implicit AND logic.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateEntityManagement
member.summary:
Demonstrates entity management and structure discovery capabilities.
This method showcases: - Listing all available entities in the data source - Inspecting entity structures and field definitions - Creating custom entity structures programmatically - Setting primary keys and field properties - Adding data to newly created entities
The entity management system supports automatic schema discovery from data objects as well as explicit entity structure definition.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.Cleanup
member.summary:
Performs proper cleanup and resource disposal.
This method demonstrates: - Proper connection closure - Resource disposal patterns - Exception handling during cleanup - Logging of cleanup operations
Following proper cleanup patterns is essential for memory management and preventing resource leaks in production applications.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.RunAdvancedExample
member.summary:
Demonstrates advanced cache operations with external cache provider integration.
This method showcases: - Custom cache configuration with specific settings - Integration with CacheManager for advanced scenarios - Direct cache provider access and manipulation - Performance testing and benchmarking - Statistics collection and analysis - Memory usage optimization techniques
The advanced example shows how to leverage the full power of the caching system for high-performance, production-ready applications.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.DemonstrateCacheIntegration
member.summary:
Demonstrates integration between InMemoryCacheDataSource and CacheManager.
This method shows: - Dual access to cached data through different interfaces - Cache key naming conventions and patterns - Cross-system data accessibility - Integration verification and validation
The integration allows data stored through InMemoryCacheDataSource to be accessible through the CacheManager API and vice versa.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Examples.CacheMemoryDataSourceExample.PerformanceTest
member.summary:
Performs comprehensive performance testing and benchmarking.
This method: - Measures insert performance for large datasets (1000+ records) - Tests filtering performance with complex conditions - Collects and reports cache statistics - Analyzes hit/miss ratios and memory usage - Provides performance metrics for optimization
Performance testing is essential for understanding the scalability and efficiency characteristics of the caching system.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Caching.Providers.SimpleCacheProvider
member.summary:
Simple, working in-memory cache provider optimized for .NET 8/9.
This provider serves as the foundational, lightweight caching solution with essential features:
**Core Capabilities:** - Thread-safe operations using ConcurrentDictionary for high concurrency - Automatic expiration with configurable TTL (Time To Live) settings - JSON-based serialization for universal data type support - Background cleanup timer for expired item removal - Comprehensive statistics tracking (hits, misses, memory usage) - Simple LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction when capacity limits are reached
**Performance Characteristics:** - Optimized for moderate-scale applications (up to ~10,000 items) - Low memory overhead with efficient data structures - Fast access times for cached items (microsecond range) - Minimal CPU overhead for background maintenance
**Use Cases:** - Development and testing environments - Small to medium applications with basic caching needs - Fallback provider for more complex caching scenarios - Applications requiring simple, reliable caching without external dependencies - Microservices with lightweight caching requirements
**Configuration Options:** - Default expiry time for cached items - Maximum item count before eviction - Cleanup interval for expired item removal - Key prefix for namespacing - Statistics collection enable/disable
**Thread Safety:** All operations are thread-safe and can handle high concurrency scenarios without requiring external synchronization mechanisms.
**Memory Management:** Uses efficient memory allocation patterns and provides automatic cleanup of expired items to prevent memory leaks in long-running applications.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.FileDataSource
member.summary: Connection lifecycle partial — open, close, transactions, query routing. All format-specific work is delegated to .
CRUD partial — Insert, Update, Delete, bulk UpdateEntities. All file write operations are delegated to .

Format-agnostic file data source. The actual file parsing is delegated to an resolved at connection-open time via using .
Supported formats (built-in): CSV, TSV, JSON, Text. Additional formats can be registered with .
Diagnostics / validation partial — ValidateRow, CanConvert, dead-letter helpers.
Governance partial — access policy enforcement, row-level security, field masking. Phase 7 implementation.
Query / read partial — GetEntity, paging, async, GetScalar. Delegates all file parsing to the set in Connection.cs. Filter evaluation is format-agnostic (works on projected string values).
Reader host surface for listing/switching file readers at runtime.

Schema inference partial — GetEntityStructure, CreateEntityAs, CreateEntities. Delegates header reading and type inference to . Phase 3: uses confidence-scored for per-column type and quality statistics.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry
member.summary:
Discovers, caches, and auto-registers implementations that are decorated with .
Uses IDMEEditor.assemblyHandler.ConfigEditor.FileReaderClasses for discovery — the same mechanism as [AddinAttribute] connectors and [PipelinePluginAttribute] ETL plugins.
Calling also calls for each found reader so that all existing code that calls FileReaderFactory.GetReader(type) continues to work transparently.
uses for core readers.
Typical usage:
var registry = new FileReaderRegistry(editor); registry.Discover(); // finds all [FileReader(…)]-attributed classes var reader = registry.Create(DataSourceType.Parquet);

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.RegisterAttributedReadersFromAssembly(System.Reflection.Assembly)
member.summary: Registers all attributed file readers from a given assembly. Used by before an editor exists.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.Discover
member.summary:
Scans all assemblies loaded by AssemblyHandler and registers every concrete class that implements and carries a .
Each discovered reader is also registered in so existing code that calls FileReaderFactory.GetReader(type) works without changes.
Safe to call multiple times — existing descriptors are updated (re-discovery).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.Create(TheTechIdea.Beep.Utilities.DataSourceType)
member.summary: Creates a fresh instance of the reader registered for . The reader is created with its default (no-argument) constructor — readers do not require IDMEEditor.
member.exception: No plugin was discovered for the given .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.FileManager.Registry.FileReaderRegistry.GetAll
member.summary: Returns all currently discovered descriptors.

member.summary: Returns true if a plugin for is registered.

member.summary: Number of discovered plugins.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.DataView.DataViewDataSource
member.summary: Represents a data source for a data view.
Partial: Entity Lifecycle operations — move, rename, duplicate, reorder, get by name.
Partial: Field/Column management — add, remove, get, refresh schema.
Partial: Filter Management — per-entity WHERE filters stored in EntityStructure.CustomBuildQuery.
Partial: Join Graph Intelligence — auto-detect joins, shortest path, adjacency list.
Partial: Query and execution — preview, cache status, async materialize, execution plan.
Partial: Validation and health-check operations.
Partial: View-level utilities — clone, merge, datasource list, summary.

member.summary: Phase 1 — Basic full sync from SourceDB.Customers to DestDB.Customers.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.BeepSync.Examples.Example02_SchemaVersioning
member.summary: Phase 2 — Schema versioning, approval-state lifecycle, and drift detection.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.BeepSync.Examples.Example03_IncrementalSync
member.summary: Phase 3 — Incremental sync using a timestamp watermark (Upsert mode). Runs repeated delta syncs, each picking up only records changed since the previous run.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.BeepSync.Examples.Example04_BidirectionalConflict
member.summary: Phase 4 — Two-way sync of a Products entity with last-write-wins conflict resolution. After the run the conflict evidence list is inspected for audit purposes.

member.summary: Phase 5 — Large-table sync with retry on transient errors and checkpoint-based resume.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.BeepSync.Examples.Example06_DataQualityAndReconciliation
member.summary: Phase 6 — Sync with DQ gate rules, reject routing, and reconciliation reporting.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.BeepSync.Examples.Example07_SloAndAlerting
member.summary: Phase 7 — SLO-enforced sync with alert rules and structured run metrics. Shows how to wire a Rule Engine via SyncIntegrationContext so alert rules fire.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.BeepSync.Examples.Example08_PerformanceAndParallel
member.summary: Phase 8 — High-throughput sync configuration and parallel fan-out of multiple schemas.

Phase notes embedded in summary tags show when each member was added to the plan.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.IResolverCapabilities
member.summary: Describes the runtime capabilities and scheduling metadata of a resolver. Implemented by resolvers that want to declare priority or caching hints. Base class BaseDefaultValueResolver supplies safe defaults when not implemented.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.IResolverCapabilities.Priority
member.summary: Lower numbers are tried first when multiple resolvers claim a rule. Default: 100.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.IResolverCapabilities.IsDeterministic
member.summary: True when the same rule + context always produces the same value. Non-deterministic resolvers (RANDOM, volatile NOW) must return false. Cache (Phase 6) skips storing results when this is false.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.IResolverCapabilities.SupportsCaching
member.summary: True when the resolver's results are safe to cache across calls.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.IResolverCapabilities.SupportsAsync
member.summary: Future-ready flag — true when the resolver can execute asynchronously.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.ResolverContext
member.summary: Enriched resolution context passed through the resolver pipeline. Wraps and carries per-call extras so resolvers never need to cast the raw args to reach entity/field names.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.ResolverContext.PassedArgs
member.summary: Original framework arguments.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Interfaces.ResolverContext.Extras

member.summary: Convenience shortcut — current entity from .

member.summary: Field name resolved from Extras["FieldName"] first, then as fallback.

member.summary: Returns the extra value cast to , or the default when absent.

Each discovered resolver is also registered into (via RegisterCustomResolver) so existing code that calls DefaultsManager.Apply(…) works without any changes.
Safe to call multiple times — existing descriptors are updated (re-discovery).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver
member.summary:
Resolver for getting default values from data sources.
All operations use + exclusively so they work across every IDataSource implementation — not just RDBMS. Aggregates (COUNT/MAX/MIN/SUM/AVG) are computed in memory via LINQ on the result set. Filter values prefixed with '@' are bound from runtime context parameters.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver.HandleGetEntity(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs)
member.summary: GETENTITY(entityName[, field=value ...]) Returns the first matching entity object.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver.HandleLookup(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs)
member.summary: LOOKUP(entityName, fieldToReturn[, field=value ...]) ENTITYLOOKUP(entityName, fieldToReturn[, field=value ...]) Returns the value of a specific field from the first matching entity.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver.HandleQuery(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs)
member.summary:
QUERY(mode, ...) — universal entity-based query using AppFilter.
Modes: scalar QUERY(scalar, entityName, fieldName[, filter...]) → value of fieldName from the first matching record first QUERY(first, entityName[, filter...]) → first matching entity object exists QUERY(exists, entityName[, filter...]) → true when at least one record matches, false otherwise count QUERY(count, entityName[, filter...]) → number of matching records aggregate QUERY(aggregate, func, entityName, fieldName[, filter...]) → MAX / MIN / SUM / AVG over fieldName across matching records

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver.HandleAggregate(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs,System.String)
member.summary: COUNT(entityName[, filter...]) MAX / MIN / SUM / AVG (entityName, fieldName[, filter...])

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver.ParseFilterCondition(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs)
member.summary: Parses "Field=Value", "Field!=Value", etc. Values prefixed with '@' are resolved from runtime context.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.Resolvers.DataSourceResolver.BindParameter(System.String,TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs)
member.summary: Resolves '@Param' tokens against runtime context. Checks (in order): named Objects list → record fields → IPassedArgs string slots. Returns the original string when no binding is found.

member.summary: Resolver for date/time related default values with enhanced functionality

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.DotStyleRuleParser
member.summary:
Parses rule strings that use the dot-segment DSL syntax (DSL v1).
General form: Operator.arg1.arg2...argN
Segmentation rules: - The first dot-delimited token is the operator (case-insensitive). - Subsequent tokens are arguments in order. - A segment may be quoted with single or double quotes to protect embedded dots: QUERY.scalar.'SELECT a.b FROM t' - Decimal numbers are kept intact: ADD.10.5 → args ["10","5"] - Comparison expressions that include dots (e.g. 1.5) must be quoted.
The parser sets on success.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.DotStyleRuleParser.IsDotStyleRule(System.String)
member.summary: Heuristically decides whether a rule string uses the dot-style DSL. The rule must start with a known operator followed immediately by a dot, OR be a known no-argument operator token (like "NOW", "TODAY", "GUID").

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.DotStyleRuleParser.Parse(System.String)
member.summary: Parses the given dot-style rule string into a .
member.returns: A — check before use.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.DotStyleRuleParser.IsDecimalDot(System.Text.StringBuilder,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true when the dot at is a decimal-number separator, e.g. the buffer ends with a digit and the next character is a digit.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.DotStyleRuleParser.BuildNormalized(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Converts the operator + args back to a function-style string so existing resolvers can process it without modification. e.g. IF.Age>=18.Adult.Minor → IF(Age>=18,Adult,Minor)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.DotStyleRuleParser.EscapeArg(System.String)
member.summary: Adds quotes around an argument if it contains a comma or parenthesis so the normalized form does not confuse the legacy SplitParameters helper.

member.summary: Identifies which DSL syntax was used to write the rule.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleSyntaxVersion.V1Legacy
member.summary: Legacy function-call style: IF(cond, trueVal, falseVal)

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleSyntaxVersion.V1Dot
member.summary: Dot-segment style (DSL v1): IF.cond.trueVal.falseVal

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleSyntaxVersion.Unknown
member.summary: Unknown or unparseable syntax.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleSyntaxVersion.Literal
member.summary: The string has no leading `:` identifier and is treated as a plain literal value. No resolver will be invoked; the value is used as-is.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleDiagnosticSeverity
member.summary: Severity level of a rule parse diagnostic message.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleDiagnostic
member.summary: A diagnostic message produced when parsing or validating a rule string.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleDiagnostic.Severity
member.summary: Severity of the issue.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleDiagnostic.Code
member.summary: Short error code, e.g. "DSL001".

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleDiagnostic.Message
member.summary: Human-readable description of the issue.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleDiagnostic.Position
member.summary: Index in the original rule string where the problem was detected (-1 = unknown).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule
member.summary: Canonical internal representation of a parsed default-value rule. Both legacy function-style rules and dot-style DSL rules are normalised into this structure so that resolvers and validators can work uniformly.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.Operator
member.summary: Uppercase operator name extracted from the rule, e.g. "IF", "QUERY", "NOW", "ADD". Empty string when the rule could not be parsed.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.Args
member.summary: Ordered list of argument strings extracted from the rule. For dot-style rules these are the dot-separated segments after the operator. For legacy rules these are the comma-separated parameters inside parentheses.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.OriginalRule
member.summary: Verbatim original rule string supplied by the caller.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.NormalizedRule
member.summary: Equivalent legacy function-style representation produced by the normalizer, suitable for routing to existing resolvers. e.g. dot-style "IF.Age>=18.Adult.Minor" → "IF(Age>=18,Adult,Minor)"

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.SyntaxVersion
member.summary: Which DSL syntax was detected.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.IsValid
member.summary: True when no Error-severity diagnostics were produced.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.IsLiteral
member.summary: True when this rule is a plain literal value (no leading `:` identifier). When true, holds the literal value and no resolver should be invoked.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.Diagnostics
member.summary: Parse and validation diagnostics (may include Info, Warning, or Error entries).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.ParsedRule.ToString
member.summary: Returns the normalized rule string.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleNormalizer
member.summary:
Entry-point for rule normalization.
Given any rule string (legacy function-style OR new dot-style DSL), returns a whose is always a legacy function-style string that existing resolvers can consume unchanged.
Usage:
var parsed = RuleNormalizer.Normalize(rule); if (!parsed.IsValid) { /* handle diagnostics */ } string routedRule = parsed.NormalizedRule; // feed to resolver

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Defaults.RuleParsing.RuleNormalizer._legacyExpressionTokens
member.summary: Parses , detects the syntax version, and returns a canonical with a resolver-ready .
member.returns: A whose flag indicates whether normalisation succeeded. Diagnostics are available on the returned object.

member.summary: Internal helper: normalizes an expression string (already stripped of the `:` prefix) using the dot-style parser or legacy function-style path.

member.summary: Convenience: normalizes the rule and returns the normalized string to route to resolvers. Returns the original rule unchanged when normalization fails.
member.param: Receives a diagnostics summary string on failure, or null on success.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOWManager.FormsManager
member.summary: Message and alert built-ins partial class. Provides Oracle Forms MESSAGE / SHOW_ALERT / BELL equivalents.
FormsManager partial — Phase 5 Audit Trail & Change Tracking. Subscribes to and exposes audit query / export / maintenance APIs.
Block property built-ins partial class. Exposes Oracle Forms SET_BLOCK_PROPERTY / GET_BLOCK_PROPERTY semantics.

Enhanced UnitofWorksManager with modular design using helper classes. Simulates Oracle Forms environment with master-detail relationships, triggers, and form-level operations management.
This is the main coordinator class - complex operations are delegated to: - Helper classes for specific functionality - Partial classes for related operations - The main class stays lean and focused on coordination
Phase 3 + 4 data-operation facade methods delegating to UOW marker interfaces.
Phase 4.2 — DML Trigger wrappers. Provides , , helpers that fire the new / / triggers added in Phase 4. Also provides the Oracle Forms RAISE_FORM_TRIGGER built-in via .
Enhanced operations partial class for UnitofWorksManager Provides enhanced operations and error recovery with mode transition awareness
Form-level operations partial class for UnitofWorksManager

Generic and LOV operations partial class for FormsManager. Provides type-safe block registration, typed record accessors and LOV display helpers. Phase 1 — 1.1 Generic Block Registration / 1.4 LOV Full Integration.
Phase 4.1 — Key Trigger wrappers. Provides / helpers that map values to matching enum values on the underlying . Also wires default keyboard actions: KEY-ENTER → commit, KEY-EXIT → close, etc.

Mode Transition Management partial class for UnitofWorksManager Handles transitions between Query and CRUD modes with proper validation Equivalent to Oracle Forms ENTER_QUERY / EXECUTE_QUERY mode transitions
Navigation operations partial class for UnitofWorksManager
FormsManager partial — Phase 7 Performance & Scalability. Provides paging, lazy-load configuration, and cache management APIs.
FormsManager partial — Phase 6 Security & Authorization. Exposes security context management, block/field security registration, field masking and violation logging.
Sequence and default-value built-ins partial class. Provides Oracle Forms :SEQUENCE.NEXTVAL, DEFAULT_VALUE, COPY, and group-populate patterns.

Timer management built-ins partial class. Exposes Oracle Forms CREATE_TIMER / DELETE_TIMER / GET_TIMER equivalents. Timer expiry fires the TriggerType.WhenTimerExpired trigger on the current form (see OnTimerManagerFired in FormsManager.cs core).
Phase 4.3 — Trigger chaining, dependency ordering, and execution logging. Subscribes to to populate an in-memory , and exposes dependency-ordered fire helpers via .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.PagingManager
member.summary: Manages per-block paging state for virtual scrolling — Phase 7. Does not load data itself; callers re-execute their query after calling .

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.DefaultFieldMaskProvider
member.summary:
Default field-mask provider. Pattern rules:
* (single asterisk) — replaces the whole value with "*****".
Any other pattern — '#' matches a digit, '*' matches any character; unmatched positions in the raw value are replaced with the next pattern character.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.SecurityManager
member.summary: Manages block- and field-level authorization for the forms engine. Evaluates the current against registered and rules.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.SequenceProvider
member.summary: In-memory implementation of ISequenceProvider. Provides named auto-increment counters that persist for the lifetime of the FormsManager. Corresponds to Oracle Forms :SEQUENCE.NEXTVAL usage patterns. Thread-safe via Interlocked operations.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.SharedBlockManager
member.summary: Manages data blocks shared across multiple FormsManager instances. Provides optimistic locking so only one form modifies a shared block at a time.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.SharedBlockManager.NotifySharedBlockChanged(System.String,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Notify subscribers that a shared block's data has changed. Call this after committing changes to any shared block.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.TimerManager
member.summary: Manages named form-level timers that fire WHEN-TIMER-EXPIRED triggers. Corresponds to Oracle Forms CREATE_TIMER / DELETE_TIMER / GET_TIMER built-ins. Thread-safe — uses System.Threading.Timer internally.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.TriggerDependencyManager
member.summary: Topological sort and cycle detection for trigger dependency graphs. Uses DFS-based algorithm on TriggerDefinition.DependsOn lists.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Forms.Helpers.TriggerExecutionLog
member.summary: Thread-safe in-memory ring-buffer of recent trigger execution records.

Unique rule name (nullable — auto-generated if empty)

member.summary: Map a local TriggerType int value to the BeepDM TriggerType enum. Local ranges: Form=1-6, Block=100-109, Record=200-214, Item=300-311, Nav=400-403, Error=500-502, Extra=600-604 BeepDM ranges: Form=0-8, Block=20-39, Record=50-54, Item=70-99, Nav=100-119, etc.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.DataImportManager
member.summary: Core functionality partial class for DataImportManager Contains data source operations, entity management, and data fetching logic

Enhanced DataImportManager with helper-based architecture and DefaultsManager integration Supports advanced data import operations with validation, transformation, batch processing, and progress monitoring

Phase 3 — Preflight validation and sync-draft generation. Logic previously lived in ImportExportOrchestrator (UI layer); moved here so all callers can use it without taking a dependency on the Winform project.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.DataImportManager.ReplayFailedRecordsAsync(System.String,System.IProgress{TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.IPassedArgs},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replays all pending (non-replayed) error records for . Each pending record is re-submitted through the configured quality checks and transformation pipeline and, if it now passes, is written to the destination. Successfully replayed records are marked with = true in the error store.

member.summary: Attempts to re-apply quality rules and write a single error record to the destination. Returns true on success.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.Factories.LocalStoreFactory
member.summary:
Creates the correct store implementations based on what is available in the environment.
When a local-DB capable driver (SQLite / LiteDB) is registered → returns DataSource-backed stores.
Otherwise → returns JSON-file-backed stores (zero-config default).
All stores are created with so that they can resolve the connection name set in .

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.Factories.LocalStoreFactory.LocalStoreConnectionName
member.summary: Name of the default local connection used by the DataSource-backed stores. Must match a registered connection in editor.ConfigEditor.DataConnections. Callers may change this before calling any Create* method.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.Factories.LocalStoreFactory.CreateWatermarkStore(TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.IDMEEditor)
member.summary: Creates the appropriate .
member.remarks: Watermarks are tiny per-pipeline key-value pairs. File-based storage is always preferred — it requires no schema and is safe across all environments.

member.summary: Creates the appropriate .

member.summary: Creates the appropriate .

member.summary: Returns true when at least one local-DB driver (SQLite / LiteDB) is registered.

member.summary: Controls whether raw records are written to a staging entity before normalization. When staging is enabled the pipeline writes all source records to <EntityName><StagingEntitySuffix> first, then normalizes and moves them to the final destination entity.

member.summary: Master switch. When false (default) records go directly to the destination.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.Staging.StagingOptions.StagingEntitySuffix
member.summary: Suffix appended to the entity name to form the staging entity name. Defaults to "_raw" (e.g. Customers_raw).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.Staging.StagingOptions.DropStagingAfterNormalize
member.summary: When true the staging entity is dropped after normalization succeeds. When false the staging entity is kept for auditing.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Importing.Staging.StagingOptions.SkipNormalization
member.summary: Skip the normalization step entirely — records remain in the staging entity only. Useful for inspection runs without writing to the live destination table.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Mapping.AutoObjMapper
member.summary: Core AutoObjMapper class - Main entry point for object mapping operations. Lightweight object mapper inspired by popular mappers (AutoMapper/TinyMapper). Goals: - Fast: expression-compiled assignment actions cached per (TSource, TDest) - Safe: nullable/enum conversions, optional ignore-null behavior - Flexible: per type-pair configuration (ignore members, custom resolvers, before/after hooks) - Zero dependencies, works with plain POCOs
AutoObjMapper - Expression Builder functionality Handles the compilation of mapping expressions
AutoObjMapper - Factory and Builder functionality Provides factory methods and builder pattern for creating mappers
AutoObjMapper - Performance and Monitoring functionality
AutoObjMapper - Validation and Error Handling functionality

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.MigrationManifestParseError.Code
member.summary: MIG-MANIFEST-001 = invalid segment count (not 1–3). MIG-MANIFEST-002 = empty type name segment. MIG-MANIFEST-003 = unresolved type after resolution pipeline. MIG-MANIFEST-004 = duplicate entity declaration.

member.summary: Result of parsing a migration manifest file.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.MigrationReportChannel

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.EntityDecisionRecord.CapabilityContextSnapshot
member.summary: Key capability facts used to reach this decision (e.g. SupportsSchemaEvolution=true).

member.summary: DDL was generated and executed successfully.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.DdlOperationOutcome.NoOp
member.summary: No DDL was needed (entity/column already correct).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.DdlOperationOutcome.Unsupported
member.summary: The provider does not support this DDL operation.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.DdlOperationOutcome.Emulated
member.summary: DDL was emulated via non-DDL means (e.g. file mutation, table rebuild).

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.DdlOperationOutcome.Failed
member.summary: DDL failed.

member.summary: File-mutation path for file-based providers.

member.name: F:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.DdlHelperSource.Direct
member.summary: Direct datasource API — no helper DDL generated.

member.summary: SHA256 prefix of the generated SQL (empty when no SQL was produced).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.DdlOperationEvidence.ReasonCode

member.summary: Ranked source category for an assembly contributed to entity discovery.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.MigrationManager
member.summary: Core MigrationManager shell. Feature groups are implemented in dedicated partial files.

Parses migration manifest .txt files that declare entity types for filesystem-based discovery (source = ).
Line format (pipe-delimited, 1–3 segments): Full.Type.Name[|AssemblyHint[|NamespacePrefix]]
Comments (#) and empty lines are silently ignored.
Error codes:
MIG-MANIFEST-001 — invalid segment count (not 1..3)
MIG-MANIFEST-002 — empty type name segment
MIG-MANIFEST-003 — unresolved type after resolution pipeline
MIG-MANIFEST-004 — duplicate entity declaration

member.summary: Register multiple assemblies for entity type discovery.

member.summary: Gets all currently registered assemblies (manual + auto-discovered). Useful for diagnostics when entity types are not being found.

member.summary: Returns a versioned recommendation profile for the resolved datasource type/category. Uses the current when parameters are null.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.Migration.MigrationManager.GetDiscoveryEvidence
member.summary: Returns the assembly discovery evidence snapshot from the most recent DiscoverEntityTypes call. Returns null if no discovery has run yet in this session.

member.summary: Returns all DDL operation evidence accumulated in this session.

member.summary: Gets all searchable assemblies from multiple sources: 1. Manually registered assemblies (RegisterAssembly / RegisterAssemblies) 2. Entry assembly and all its referenced assemblies (covers projects referenced by the exe) 3. All loaded assemblies from AppDomain.CurrentDomain 4. DMEEditor's assembly handler (plugin assemblies) This ensures entity classes from other projects loaded in the exe are always found.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOW.UnitofWork`1
member.summary: Core partial class for UnitofWork - Contains core properties, constructors and basic functionality This is the refactored version with helper-based architecture and DefaultsManager integration
Additional CRUD operations and async methods partial class for UnitofWork
Utility methods partial class for UnitofWork - Contains utility methods that were missing from the refactored version
CRUD Operations partial class for UnitofWork with DefaultsManager integration Handles Create, Read, Update, Delete operations with enhanced default value support

OBL Integration partial class for UnitofWork. Contains all passthrough methods/properties that delegate to ObservableBindingList's enhanced capabilities (Validation, Undo/Redo, Computed, Bookmarks, ThreadSafety, Freeze, BatchUpdate, Aggregates, Navigation enhancements, Master-Detail).

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOW.UnitofWork`1.FilteredUnits
member.summary: Gets or sets the filtered units.
member.remarks: This property represents a collection of units that have been filtered based on certain criteria. When setting the value, the property will unsubscribe from the previous collection's PropertyChanged event and CollectionChanged event, if applicable. It will then subscribe to the new collection's PropertyChanged event and CollectionChanged event, if applicable.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOW.UnitofWork`1.Units
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of units.
member.remarks: If the filter is applied, the filtered units collection will be returned. Otherwise, the original units collection will be returned.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOW.UnitofWork`1.EnsureUnitsInitialized
member.summary: Ensures the _units collection is initialized. Called at the end of every constructor to guarantee Units is never null, even if OpenDataSource() failed or Validateall() returned false. This allows Add()/New() to work on a freshly constructed UnitofWork without requiring Get() to be called first.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOW.UnitofWork`1.init
member.summary: Initializes the object
member.remarks: This method performs initialization tasks for the object. It first validates all necessary conditions using the Validateall() method. If the validation fails, the method returns without performing any further initialization. If the validation succeeds, the method proceeds to clear any existing data using the Clear() method.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Editor.UOW.UnitofWork`1.getPrimaryKey(`0)
member.summary: Determines the primary key of a document
member.remarks: If the primary key is already set, this method does nothing. Otherwise, it attempts to find the primary key property of the document using the provided primary key name. If the primary key property is found, it checks if its type is string and sets the IsPrimaryKeyString flag accordingly.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Notifiers.EmailNotifier
member.summary:
Sends alert notifications via SMTP email.
Config parameters:
SmtpHost — SMTP server hostname (required)
SmtpPort — port, default 587
UseTls — enable STARTTLS, default true
Username — SMTP auth username (supports ${env:VAR})
Password — SMTP auth password (supports ${env:VAR})
From — sender address
To — JSON array of recipient addresses, or comma-separated
Subject — subject template (token-substituted)
BodyTemplate — body template (token-substituted)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Notifiers.EmailNotifier.Resolve(System.Object)
member.summary: Resolves ${env:VAR_NAME} references to environment variables.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Notifiers.LogFileNotifier
member.summary:
Appends alert notifications to a rotating local log file.
Config parameters:
FilePath — destination log file path (required)
MaxSizeMb — rotate when file exceeds this size in MB, default 50
Format — log line template (token-substituted)

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Notifiers.WebhookNotifier
member.summary:
Fires an HTTP webhook when an alert triggers. Supports Slack, Teams, and generic JSON webhooks.
Config parameters:
Url — webhook URL (required; supports ${env:VAR})
Method — "POST" | "PUT", default POST
Headers — JSON object of additional headers
PayloadTemplate — JSON body template with {Token} substitutions
HmacSecret — optional HMAC-SHA256 secret for signing (supports ${env:VAR})
TimeoutSeconds — request timeout, default 15

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.CronScheduler
member.summary:
Fires a pipeline on a CRON schedule. Supports standard 5-part CRON expressions: min hour dom mon dow. Also accepts 6-part expressions with a leading seconds field.
Config parameters:
CronExpression — e.g. "0 2 * * *" (required)
PipelineId — pipeline to trigger (injected by SchedulerHost)
TimeZone — IANA or Windows tz name, default "UTC"
MaxMissedRuns — if host was down, fire at most N missed executions (default 1)

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.EventBusScheduler
member.summary:
Fires a pipeline when a named event is published on .
Config parameters:
EventTopic — the bus topic to subscribe to (required)
PipelineId — pipeline to trigger (injected by SchedulerHost)
Filter — optional string; payload must contain this substring

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.FileWatchScheduler
member.summary:
Fires a pipeline whenever files matching a pattern appear in (or change inside) a directory.
Config parameters:
WatchPath — directory to watch (required)
PipelineId — pipeline to trigger (injected by SchedulerHost)
FilePattern — e.g. "*.csv", default "*"
Recursive — watch sub-directories, default false
TriggerOn — "Created" | "Changed" | "Both", default "Created"
Debounce_ms — milliseconds between the first event and the trigger (default 2000)
StabilityMs — additional wait to confirm the file is fully written (default 500)
PassFilePath — inject __trigger_file into Parameters, default true

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.ManualScheduler
member.summary:
A no-op scheduler that never fires on its own. Pipelines using this scheduler can only be triggered programmatically via SchedulerHost.TriggerManualAsync or by calling .
Config parameters:
PipelineId — pipeline to trigger (injected by SchedulerHost)

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.ManualScheduler.FireAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Fires the event immediately with optional override parameters. Can be called directly when the SchedulerHost is not managing this scheduler.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.PipelineDependencyScheduler
member.summary:
Fires a pipeline after one or more upstream pipelines have completed. The delegates completion tracking to DependencyGraph; this scheduler type is used primarily as a marker so that the host knows to skip active scheduling (dependency-driven pipelines are enqueued by the host itself after upstream completion).
Config parameters:
PipelineId — pipeline to trigger (injected by SchedulerHost)
DependsOn — JSON array of schedule IDs, e.g. ["schedA","schedB"]
Condition — ALL_SUCCESS | ANY_SUCCESS | ALL_COMPLETE, default ALL_SUCCESS

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.PipelineDependencyScheduler.DependsOn
member.summary: Schedule IDs that must complete before this scheduler may fire.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.PipelineDependencyScheduler.Condition
member.summary: Completion condition: ALL_SUCCESS | ANY_SUCCESS | ALL_COMPLETE.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.PipelineDependencyScheduler.StartAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: No active polling — the SchedulerHost drives this scheduler via DependencyGraph.GetUnblockedSchedules() after each upstream completion.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Schedulers.PipelineDependencyScheduler.FireDependencyMet(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Called by SchedulerHost when the dependency graph reports this schedule is unblocked. Fires directly.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Sinks.DataSinkPlugin
member.summary: Built-in sink that writes records into any IDataSource registered with BeepDM. Parameters: DataSourceName (string, required) — registered connection name. EntityName (string, required) — target table / entity. WriteMode (string, optional) — Insert | Update | Upsert (default: Insert).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Sinks.ErrorLogSinkPlugin
member.summary: Built-in sink that writes rejected / invalid records to a newline-delimited JSON file at {BeepDataPath}/Pipelines/Errors/{runId}.errors.jsonl. Intended to be attached to the error outlet of the validation step. Parameters: none required. Optional — MaxErrors (int, default 10 000).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Sources.CsvSourcePlugin
member.summary: Built-in source plugin that reads records from a CSV file via the BeepDM CSV data source registered under the given connection name. Parameters: FilePath (string, required) — absolute path to the CSV file. ConnectionName (string, optional) — if a CSV data source is already registered, use it. HasHeader (bool, optional, default true). Delimiter (string, optional, default ",").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Sources.DataSourcePlugin
member.summary: Built-in source plugin that reads from any IDataSource registered with BeepDM. Parameters: DataSourceName (string, required) — registered connection name. EntityName (string, required) — table / entity name. Filter (string, optional) — semicolon-delimited "Field:Op:Value" triples. BatchSize (int, optional, default 1000).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.AggregateTransformer
member.summary:
Groups records by one or more fields and emits aggregated rows. All input is buffered in-memory before the first output row is emitted. Parameters:
GroupBy — comma-separated field names to group by.
Aggregates — JSON object, e.g. { "TotalAmt": "SUM(Amount)", "RowCount": "COUNT(*)", "MaxSal": "MAX(Salary)" }. Supported functions: SUM COUNT AVG MAX MIN FIRST LAST.
MaxGroupCount — (int) Maximum groups to hold in memory. Default 1,000,000. 0 = unlimited.
OverflowStrategy — "Fail" (default) throws when limit is hit; "DropOldest" evicts the least-recently-seen group.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.DeDuplicateTransformer
member.summary:
Removes duplicate records within a sliding window keyed on one or more fields. Parameters:
KeyFields — comma-separated list of field names that form the key.
Strategy — KeepFirst (default), KeepLast, KeepMax(fieldName), KeepMin(fieldName).
WindowSize — max distinct keys to track (default 100 000). When exceeded, the oldest 10 % of entries are evicted.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.ExpressionTransformer
member.summary: Adds or replaces fields using computed expressions. Configuration parameter: Expressions — of { destinationFieldName: "expression string" }.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.FieldMapTransformer
member.summary: Renames and/or reorders fields. Parameter: Mappings — where key = destination field name, value = source field name. Fields not listed in Mappings are dropped from the output.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.FilterTransformer
member.summary:
Rows-level filter: retains or discards records based on a boolean expression. Parameters:
Expression — predicate string, e.g. "Country == 'US' AND Amount > 100".
Mode — "Keep" (default) passes matching rows; "Reject" drops them.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.LookupTransformer
member.summary:
Enriches records by looking up values from a reference data source. Maintains an LRU in-memory cache to minimize data-source round trips. Parameters:
LookupSource — connection name for the lookup data source.
LookupEntity — entity/table name in that source.
LookupKey — field name in the lookup entity (the column to match).
InputKey — field name in the incoming record (the value to look up).
OutputFields — JSON object { "lookupField": "destField" }.
OnMiss — "Null" (default), "Reject", or "Default".
CacheSize — max entries in the LRU cache (default 1000).
PreLoad — true to load the entire lookup table at startup (ignores CacheSize during pre-load).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.ScriptTransformer
member.summary: Inline C# script transformer — stub implementation. Currently acts as a pass-through; Roslyn-based compilation will be added in a later phase. Configure the Script parameter with the C# snippet to be compiled and applied per-record.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.SplitTransformer
member.summary:
Tags each record with a route label written to Meta["__route_tag"], enabling downstream fan-out to different sinks based on data content. Routes are evaluated in declaration order; the first matching route wins. Unmatched records receive an empty route tag and are passed through. Parameters:
Routes — JSON array of { "Tag": "...", "Filter": "expr" }.
DefaultTag — Tag assigned to unmatched records. Default is empty string.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Transformers.TypeCastTransformer
member.summary: Casts field values to specified CLR types using . Parameter: Casts — where key = field name, value = CLR type name (e.g. "System.Int32", "System.DateTime"). Fields not listed in Casts pass through unchanged. On conversion failure the original value is preserved and a warning is logged.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Validators.CustomExpressionValidator
member.summary:
Evaluates one or more expression-based rules against each record. Rules are evaluated in order; the first failure determines the overall outcome. Parameters:
Rules — JSON array of { "Expression": "...", "Message": "...", "Outcome": "Reject|Warn" }. Expression must evaluate to a boolean; a true result means the record passes the rule.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Validators.NotNullValidator
member.summary:
Rejects records that contain null (or whitespace-only string) values in the specified fields. Parameters:
Fields — comma-separated list of field names to check.
Message — rejection message template (default: "Field '{field}' must not be null.").

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Validators.RangeValidator
member.summary:
Validates that numeric or date field values fall within a specified range. Parameters:
Rules — JSON array of { "Field": "...", "Min": "...", "Max": "..." }. Omit Min or Max to apply a one-sided range check.
Outcome — "Reject" (default) or "Warn" on violation.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Validators.ReferentialIntegrityValidator
member.summary:
Verifies that field values exist as records in a reference data source. One data-source query is made per distinct key value (LRU cache reduces round trips). Parameters:
Rules — JSON array of { "Field": "...", "LookupSource": "...", "LookupEntity": "...", "LookupField": "..." }.
CacheSize — per-rule LRU cache size (default 500).
Outcome — "Reject" (default) or "Warn" on violation.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Validators.RegexValidator
member.summary:
Validates field values against regular-expression patterns. Parameters:
Rules — JSON array of { "Field": "...", "Pattern": "...", "Message": "..." }.
Outcome — "Reject" (default) or "Warn" when a pattern does not match.
Null field values are skipped (combine with if needed).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.BuiltIn.Validators.UniquenessValidator
member.summary:
Checks that a combination of field values is unique within the current pipeline run. Parameters:
Fields — comma-separated list of field names that form the unique key.
Scope — "RunLocal" (default, tracks duplicates in-memory for this run) or a data-source connection name to query for pre-existing duplicates. Note: cross-run deduplication via a data source is expensive; prefer for large volumes.
Outcome — "Reject" (default) or "Warn" on violation.
WindowSize — max keys held in memory for RunLocal scope (default 500 000).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.Expressions.SimpleExpressionEvaluator
member.summary: Lightweight expression evaluator used by the pipeline's filter and computed-field transformers. Supports arithmetic and logical operators, string concatenation, and a set of built-in functions — all without any code-compilation dependency (no Roslyn required).
member.remarks: Supported operators: + - * / == != < > <= >= AND OR NOT. Supported functions: UPPER LOWER TRIM LEN ROUND YEAR MONTH DAY TODAY IF CONCAT SUBSTR ISNULL COALESCE. Field names that exist in the supplied are resolved automatically.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.Expressions.SimpleExpressionEvaluator.#ctor(System.String)
member.param: The raw expression string, e.g. "UPPER(FirstName) + ' ' + UPPER(LastName)".
member.exception: Thrown when the expression contains an unrecognised character.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.Expressions.SimpleExpressionEvaluator.Evaluate(TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.PipelineRecord)
member.summary: Evaluates the expression against the supplied record, returning the raw CLR value. Returns on empty expression.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.Expressions.SimpleExpressionEvaluator.Evaluate(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Evaluates the expression against a simple string-keyed dictionary (for tests or templates).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.Expressions.SimpleExpressionEvaluator.EvaluateBool(TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.PipelineRecord)
member.summary: Evaluates the expression and coerces the result to . Used by FilterTransformer and validator rules.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager
member.summary:
Orchestrates wave-based migration of pipeline definitions from legacy to enterprise-target behavior.
Responsibilities: - Persist and load definitions. - Execute canary and shadow parallel runs for comparison. - Compute per-wave and weekly . - Promote waves to Completed or trigger automatic rollback based on KPI thresholds.
Storage: {BeepDataPath}/MigrationWaves/{waveId}.wave.json Comparisons: {BeepDataPath}/MigrationWaves/Comparisons/{comparisonId}.cmp.json

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.ObservabilityStore
member.summary: Optional observability store for audit entries.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.SaveWaveAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.MigrationWave,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists a wave definition (create or update).

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.StartWaveAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Starts a wave by setting its status to and recording who promoted it.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.CompleteWaveAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Completes a wave after all health checks pass.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.RollbackWaveAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Rolls back a wave. Optionally restores each pipeline from a pre-migration snapshot stored in a .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.RunCanaryAsync(System.String,System.String,System.IProgress{TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.PassedArgs},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs both the current pipeline and a candidate pipeline in sequence (canary) and compares their output metrics. The candidate pipeline is identified by .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.RunShadowAsync(System.String,System.String,System.IProgress{TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.PassedArgs},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the candidate pipeline in parallel with the baseline. The candidate's output records are discarded — only metrics are compared. This is used to validate resource usage and error rates before full promotion.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.ComputeKpiSnapshotAsync(System.String,System.DateTime,System.DateTime,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Computes a for the pipelines in a wave over the specified time window.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.GenerateGovernanceReportAsync(System.DateTime,System.DateTime,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Generates a weekly KPI governance report across all defined waves.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.MigrationManager.EvaluateAndAutoRollbackAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Evaluates an in-progress wave's current KPIs and automatically triggers rollback if thresholds are breached.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineChannelBridge
member.summary: Bounded wrapper that decouples an async source producer from a downstream consumer. A capacity of 1 000 records is the default; tune via the constructor when many parallel steps are running.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineChannelBridge.PumpAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.PipelineRecord},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Drains into the channel, then completes the writer. Intended to run as a background via Task.Run.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineChannelBridge.ReadAllAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)

member.summary:
Persists pipeline run checkpoints to disk so that an interrupted run can be resumed from the last committed batch.
Storage location: {BeepDataPath}/Pipelines/Checkpoints/{runId}.chk.json

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineCheckpointManager.SaveAsync(TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.PipelineRunContext,System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: Snapshot current run progress to a checkpoint file. Called by the engine after each committed batch.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineDefinitionExtensions
member.summary:
Fluent builder extension methods for . Enables concise, readable pipeline construction:
var pipeline = new PipelineDefinition() .WithSource("beep.source.datasource", ("SourceDataSource", "northwind"), ("SourceEntity", "Orders")) .AddTransformer("beep.transform.typecast", ("Casts", new { Amount = "System.Decimal" })) .AddValidator("beep.validate.notnull", ("Fields", "OrderId,Amount")) .WithSink("beep.sink.datasource", ("TargetDataSource", "warehouse"), ("TargetEntity", "FactOrders")) .WithBatchSize(1000) .WithRetries(3) .Build();

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineEngine
member.summary:
Core pipeline runner. Given a it:
Resolves and configures source, transformer, validator, and sink plugins via the registry.
Streams records through each active step.
Writes accepted records to the main sink in configurable batches.
Routes rejected records to the error sink.
Saves checkpoints after each committed batch when enabled.
Records column-level lineage when enabled.
Execute a pipeline definition and return the run result.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineEngine.ResumeAsync(System.String,System.IProgress{TheTechIdea.Beep.Addin.PassedArgs},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Resume a previously interrupted run from its last checkpoint.
Internal resume entry-point called by PipelineManager.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineEngine.FilterStream(System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.PipelineRecord},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Filters using a simple boolean expression. Syntax: Field OP Value [AND|OR ...]. Supported operators: =, !=, <>, >, >=, <, <=. Numeric fields are compared numerically; all others use case-insensitive string comparison. Records that cannot be evaluated are passed through unchanged.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Engine.PipelineEngine.EvaluatePredicate(TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.PipelineRecord,System.String)
member.summary: Evaluates a single predicate of the form FieldName OP Value. Operators are checked longest-first to avoid prefix conflicts (e.g. >= before >).

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.ConcurrencyGate
member.summary: Limits the number of concurrent runs for a given pipeline using per-key semaphores. Callers acquire a slot before starting a run and release it (via ) when done. Tracks contention metrics (total waits, cumulative wait duration, active slots).

member.summary: Acquires a concurrency slot for . Waits if slots are already taken. Dispose the returned handle to release the slot.

member.summary: Returns the number of active (acquired) slots for .

member.summary: Returns contention metrics for all tracked keys.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph
member.summary: Tracks which schedules have completed and which are now unblocked based on their declared dependencies. Completion records are scoped to today's UTC date window (auto-reset at midnight). Supports deadlock detection, max-wait timeout, fail-fast propagation, and circuit breaker awareness.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.RegisterDependency(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Register or update the dependency list for .

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.SetMaxWait(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the maximum wait time in seconds for a schedule's dependencies.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.SetCircuitBroken(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Mark a schedule as circuit-breaker tripped. It will be excluded from unblocked results.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.NotifyCompletion(System.String,System.Boolean,System.DateTime)
member.summary: Record that completed.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.GetUnblockedSchedules
member.summary: Returns schedule IDs whose dependencies are now all satisfied (within today's UTC window). Each unblocked ID is returned only once per day. Excludes circuit-broken schedules and propagates fail-fast for ALL_SUCCESS failures.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.GetFailFastSchedules
member.summary: Returns schedule IDs whose upstream dependencies have fatally failed and cannot be satisfied. For ALL_SUCCESS: any upstream failure means the dependent can never succeed today. These should be marked as failed-fast.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.GetTimedOutSchedules
member.summary: Returns schedule IDs that have exceeded their max-wait time while still waiting for dependencies.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.DetectCycles
member.summary: Detects cycles in the dependency graph (deadlock detection). Returns the IDs involved in the first cycle found, or empty if none.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.GetDownstreamDependents(System.String)
member.summary: Returns all downstream schedule IDs that transitively depend on . Useful for impact analysis when a schedule fails.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.ResetDayWindow
member.summary: Reset the day window (clear completions and fired set). Call at midnight.

member.summary: Simple in-process publish/subscribe event bus used by EventBusScheduler. External code can publish named topics; schedulers subscribed to those topics fire. Thread-safe; subscriber exceptions are swallowed to protect the publisher. Maintains a bounded event history for diagnostics and audit.

member.summary: Unsubscribe from .

member.summary: Publish an event to all subscribers of . fields are forwarded as pipeline override parameters. Subscriber exceptions are caught and do not affect other subscribers. The event is recorded in the history buffer for diagnostics.

member.name: T:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.PipelineRunQueue
member.summary: Thread-safe in-memory priority queue for items with workload class isolation. Lower number = higher dispatch priority. Within the same priority, earlier is dequeued first (FIFO). Workload classes (critical, standard, backfill) are dispatched in priority order, with configurable concurrency quotas per class to prevent backfill starvation of live runs.

member.name: P:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.PipelineRunQueue.Count
member.summary: Number of runs currently waiting across all classes.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.PipelineRunQueue.CountByClass(System.String)
member.summary: Number of runs waiting in a specific workload class.

member.summary: Set the maximum concurrent dispatch limit for a workload class. 0 = unlimited.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.PipelineRunQueue.Enqueue(TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Models.QueuedRun)
member.summary: Enqueue a run for dispatch into its workload class queue.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.PipelineRunQueue.DequeueAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Awaits the next available run, dispatching in workload class priority order. Critical runs are dispatched first, then standard, then backfill. Respects per-class concurrency limits.

member.name: M:TheTechIdea.Beep.Pipelines.Scheduling.PipelineRunQueue.NotifyRunCompleted(System.String)
member.summary: Notify the queue that a run from a workload class has finished, freeing a concurrency slot for that class.

member.summary:
Core workflow runner. Executes a by:
Topologically sorting steps via the graph.
Executing each step, dispatching to the appropriate handler by .
Capturing a per step.
Applying per-step and rules.
Pausing on Approval steps and waiting for / .
